pattern: Instead of enumerating which three digits to pick, enumerate every even three-digit number directly and ask whether the available digit counts can supply it -- flipping the search from 'which combination' to 'is this candidate buildable'.
hints: There are only 900 three-digit numbers and half of those are even, so it can be cheaper to check every candidate number than to generate every combination of digits.

Count how many of each digit 0-9 are available. For a candidate even number with no leading zero, break it into its three digits and check whether the available counts can cover the digit counts the candidate needs (watching out for a digit the candidate repeats).
Walk every even number from 100 to 998, and for each one that passes the digit-count check, add it to the result -- since you're iterating in increasing order, the result comes out sorted for free.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, ten valid numbers from four distinct digits
statement example, repeated digits allow reuse up to their count
statement example, no even integer is possible at all
exactly three digits, the minimum allowed length
only zeros available, every combination has a leading zero
digits that could form a number with a leading zero must be excluded even though the digits exist
only odd digits present, no even number is possible
a single repeated digit used three times
all ten digit values available at least once

stress.description: random digit multisets of varying length and composition
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100))
    digits = [rng.randint(0, 9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [digits]

complexity.time: O(1) candidate scan (900 numbers) plus O(n) counting
